# Quickler Reports

> Chat-first reporting product for inspection, survey, site visit, compliance, and maintenance teams. Capture site notes, photos, and voice on site — Quickler structures them into a near-finished report.

## What Quickler Does

Quickler Reports removes double-handling from report writing in inspections, surveys, site visits, compliance work, and maintenance. Teams capture information on site once through familiar chat-style input; Quickler structures and drafts the report underneath, leaving the reviewer with a document that is mostly done.

**Core workflow:**
- On-site capture via chat (text, photos, voice)
- Structured report drafting from unstructured field capture
- Human review and sign-off before output
- Routing to the correct format or stakeholder

**Target users:** Inspection teams, surveyors, site visit teams, compliance teams, and maintenance teams who write the same types of reports repeatedly.

## Current Stage

Early pilot validation. Quickler is working with real technical teams to validate the first reporting niche and prove the capture-to-draft loop before full productisation.

The focus is now the reporting product itself: validating the first niche and tightening the capture-to-report loop for early teams. The current offer should be understood as a focused pilot around one repeated workflow, not a broad finished enterprise platform.

## Pilot Sign-Up

Quickler is looking for a small number of initial teams in inspections, surveys, site visits, compliance, and maintenance.

**Best pilot fit:**
- Your team writes similar inspection, survey, site visit, compliance, or maintenance reports repeatedly
- Notes, photos, voice, or forms are captured during the job
- The formal report is still rebuilt later at a desk
- You are willing to trial an early workflow and give feedback
- You can share real examples of the current report or inputs

**Primary CTA:** Apply for an early pilot at https://quickler.co/pages/contact.html

**Site goal:** The public site is designed to turn good-fit visitors into early pilot applicants. Case studies, product pages, and founder pages exist to support that decision, not as separate destination content.

## Company

**Legal name:** Quickler Ltd
**Registered:** Companies House, Scotland (registered 2026-03-11)
**Registered address:** withheld from public site
**Founder:** Philip Ross
**Operating base:** Edinburgh, Scotland, UK
**Website:** https://quickler.co/
**Contact:** philipross@quickler.co
**Demo:** https://demo.lochross.com/

## Founder

Philip Ross — BEng Electrical and Mechanical Engineering, University of Strathclyde. Previously at Dijuno, an AI analytics startup serving Coca-Cola and other major FMCG brands, where he developed expertise in LLM testing, prompt engineering, and Python automation.

**Professional memberships:** Dundee & Angus Chamber of Commerce, Federation of Small Businesses

## Background — Delivered Workflow Projects

Before Quickler Reports became the main focus, the company solved repeated admin and reporting problems in other settings. Key outcomes:

- 51.5 working days per year saved across all delivered projects
- 12× average return on time invested (hours saved per hour built)
- Systems built for charities, publishers, and commercial enterprises

Project types: purchase order workflows, fundraising and grant automation, production tracking, sales data restructuring, till data processing.

## Technologies

Python, Flask, Typst, Google Apps Script, Claude (Anthropic), Looker Studio, Docker Compose
